answer:
The verse listing the twenty promulgators of the Dharmaśāstras spans two consecutive verses in Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Chapter 1 (work slug: unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6):

Verse Yj_1.4: "Manu, Atri, Viṣṇu, Hārīta, Yājñavalkya, Uśanas, Aṅgiras, Yama, Āpastamba, Saṃvarta, Kātyāyana, and Bṛhaspati,"

Verse Yj_1.5: "Parāśara, Vyāsa, Śaṅkha, Likhita, Dakṣa, and Gautama, Śātātapa, and Vasiṣṭha are the promulgators of the Dharmaśāstras."

The twenty promulgators are: Manu, Atri, Viṣṇu, Hārīta, Yājñavalkya, Uśanas, Aṅgiras, Yama, Āpastamba, Saṃvarta, Kātyāyana, Bṛhaspati, Parāśara, Vyāsa, Śaṅkha, Likhita, Dakṣa, Gautama, Śātātapa, and Vasiṣṭha.
